title: smilax aspera subsp. mauritanica
intro: Smilax aspera subsp. mauritanica, also known as wild sarsaparilla, is a perennial climber belonging to the Smilacaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and rhizomes have been used to create herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which have been reported to provide several health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, and promoting wound healing. In addition to its medicinal properties, wild sarsaparilla has also been used as a natural dye and has been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects.
